tool.name: LDNe harmonic mean

param.name: input_data
param.label: Upload your LDNe results
param.help: Tab-delimited in txt or tabular format. The table must contain at least one of the following columns : 'NeLD', 'JK_CI_down','JK_CI_up','Overall_LD_r2','Expected_LD_r2','NeLD_corrected'.

param.name: MAF
param.label: Critical frequency values for which you wish to calculate the harmonic mean
param.help: To request multiple critical frequency values, for example: 0.02 0.05 1. The values are separated by a space.

tool.help:
Usage ===== As input, the user provides a table containing multiple populations, marker types, minor allele frequencies (MAF), and numeric statistics (e.g., NeLD, JK confidence intervals, LD r² estimates) and a comma-separated list of MAF values to process.
The tool calculates the harmonic mean for all numeric columns available in the input file for each combination of population (Pop) and marker type (marker_type), for each specified MAF. It also counts the number of subsets contributing to each estimate and includes this information in the output. Missing values (NA) are treated as infinte when computing the harmonic mean.
Numeric columns considered are : 'NeLD', 'JK_CI_down', 'JK_CI_up', 'Overall_LD_r2', 'Expected_LD_r2', 'NeLD_corrected'. Only columns present in the input file are processed.
All results are saved in a single summary table.
Example of input table
+----------+------+-------------+--------+-------+------+-------+------------+----------+----------------+ | Dataset | Pop | Marker_type | Subset | Loci | MAF | NeLD | JK_CI_down | JK_CI_up | NeLD_corrected | +==========+======+=============+========+=======+======+=======+============+==========+================+ | MyStudy | Pop1 | SNP | 1 | 1200 | 0.02 | 450.2 | 380.1 | 560.4 | 362 | +----------+------+-------------+--------+-------+------+-------+------------+----------+----------------+ | MyStudy | Pop1 | SNP | 2 | 1150 | 0.05 | 435.0 | 370.5 | 510.2 | 362 | +----------+------+-------------+--------+-------+------+-------+------------+----------+----------------+

citations.citation: @Manual{dplyr, title = {dplyr: A Grammar of Data Manipulation}, author = {Hadley Wickham and Romain François and Lionel Henry and Kirill Müller and Davis Vaughan and Posit Software, PBC}, year = {2016}, note = {R package version 0.5.0}, doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.dplyr}, url = {https://dplyr.tidyverse.org}, publisher = {Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN)}, }
